plan: 1. Create `src/ui/pngExport.ts` — a module that exports a function `initPngExport(overlay: HTMLDivElement, canvas: HTMLCanvasElement): { dispose(): void }`. It adds a 'Save PNG' button to the overlay (following the `presetSelector.ts` pattern), and on click calls `canvas.toBlob('image/png')` then triggers a download via a temporary `<a>` element with `download='latency-cathedral.png'`. The button gets a `data-action="export-png"` attribute for test targeting.

2. Create `src/domain/presetUrl.ts` — pure functions for URL↔preset serialization: `parsePresetFromUrl(search: string): PresetName | null` reads `?preset=` param and validates against known preset names, `buildPresetUrl(base: string, preset: PresetName): string` constructs a shareable URL. These stay in `src/domain/` since they are pure string→string functions with no DOM dependency.

3. Create `src/ui/shareUrl.ts` — a module that exports `initShareUrl(overlay: HTMLDivElement, presetState: PresetState): { dispose(): void }`. It adds a 'Share' button to the overlay. On click it reads `presetState.current()`, builds the URL via `buildPresetUrl`, attempts `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` wrapped in try/catch (fallback: select text in a temporary input for manual copy, or just update the URL bar). Also subscribes to preset changes and calls `history.replaceState()` to keep the URL bar in sync.

4. Update `src/main.ts` — (a) Import and call `parsePresetFromUrl(location.search)` before `createPresetState()`, passing the result as the initial preset. (b) After `initPresetSelector()`, call `initPngExport(overlay, canvas)` and `initShareUrl(overlay, presetState)`. Store dispose handles for cleanup.

5. Create `tests/unit/presetUrl.test.ts` — unit tests for `parsePresetFromUrl` (valid names return PresetName, invalid/missing returns null, handles extra params) and `buildPresetUrl` (produces correct query string, handles base URLs with existing params).

6. Create `tests/unit/pngExport.test.ts` — jsdom unit test: mock `HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.toBlob`, verify the button exists in DOM, simulate click, assert `toBlob` called with 'image/png' and a download link is created.

7. Create `tests/unit/shareUrl.test.ts` — jsdom unit test: mock `navigator.clipboard.writeText`, verify button click copies URL. Test fallback path when clipboard API throws. Test `history.replaceState` called on preset change.

8. Add E2E test `tests/e2e/export-share.spec.ts` — Playwright tests: (a) navigate to `/`, verify 'Save PNG' button visible, click it and assert a download was initiated (intercept download event). (b) Navigate to `/?preset=poor`, verify the poor preset is active on load. (c) Click a preset button, verify URL bar updates with `?preset=` param. (d) Click Share button and verify clipboard write or URL update.

9. Create `docs/adr/016-png-export-and-shareable-urls.md` — document the design decisions: using `canvas.toBlob` over `toDataURL` for memory efficiency, `history.replaceState` over `pushState` to avoid polluting back-button history, clipboard API with graceful fallback, preset-only URL params (not full snapshot serialization) to keep URLs short and stable.
10. Run full quality gate: `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test && npm run test:e2e` — fix any issues until all pass.
testing_recommendations: Unit test `parsePresetFromUrl` with valid preset names ('fast', 'mixed', 'poor'), invalid strings, empty string, missing param, and URLs with additional unrelated params
Unit test `buildPresetUrl` with various base URLs including ones that already have query params
Unit test PNG export button: mock `canvas.toBlob`, verify it's called with 'image/png' mime type on button click, and verify a download anchor element is created with correct attributes
Unit test Share URL button: mock `navigator.clipboard.writeText`, verify correct URL is copied; test the fallback path by making clipboard.writeText reject
Unit test that `history.replaceState` is called when preset selection changes, with the correct URL
E2E test: navigate to `/?preset=poor`, assert the poor preset button has the active class and the scene renders (canvas has non-zero pixels)
E2E test: click Save PNG button, intercept the download event and verify a PNG blob is produced
E2E test: select a preset, verify the URL bar updates to include `?preset=<name>`
E2E test: click Share button when clipboard is available, verify no errors thrown
E2E test: verify app loads without crash when URL has an invalid preset value like `?preset=bogus`
risks: Canvas `toBlob` may behave differently across browsers — Chromium Playwright tests won't catch Safari/Firefox quirks; mitigate by keeping the export logic simple and using the standard API
Clipboard API requires secure context (HTTPS or localhost) — dev server is localhost so tests pass, but document this constraint for production
URL param parsing must be defensive: malformed or unexpected `?preset=` values must not crash the app or select an invalid preset
If `preserveDrawingBuffer` is ever changed to false in `createRenderer.ts`, PNG export will produce blank images — add a comment documenting this dependency
History.replaceState on every preset change could interact poorly with browser extensions or analytics tools that watch for URL changes
